one of the most fun games I've ever played and i never got bored i played this game for years and still play to this day if you ever wanted to make thragg fight super man then this is your game i highly recommend (:

One of the only games i have played that is a true sandbox.
There are almost no limits beyond the 2D nature of the game, and even that can be worked around with the phase link tool. You can make amazing builds that look great, then design a bomb that flattens it instantly. You can mess around with the MANY syringes and liquids to make a super health potion that regrows limbs, or make the ultimate poison that can split somebody in half. 
You dont even have to go in depth into the game to have fun. If building Super complicated machines doesn't sound fun, you can just download them off the huge workshop. If you don't want to go into potion brewing, you can just use the presets. If you dont want to try and be a doctor, saving your ragdolls after "accidentally" throwing them a little too hard, you can just use the large arsenal of weapons (melee and ranged) to kill people in increasingly crazy ways.
Overall, this game really is what you make it. If you have any interest in sandboxes of any kind, i HIGHLY recommend this game.
I love making buildings and collapsing them with people inside. I love their screams for mercy as i shoot them in the head. 10/10 game!
You either commit murder, commit war crimes or build black holes. Truly amazing game best 2d sandbox ever.
this is THE game to let out stress, anger, or just a boredom killer. You can do just about anything to your unknowing subjects and the mods are top notch

I'de give this game a solid 8.5/10. It used to be my favourite game for a while. But, after you experiment with just about everything, the game feels finished. Though i really recommend downloading mods on steam workplace.
Best game, I ran someone over then got sentenced to 2days then went home and ran someone over in game, best game in my opinion👍

Playing this game is like letting your intrusive thoughts win; 9 times out of 10 any crazy idea that you have can be implemented with enough thought and setup.

Also the workshop implementations add even more possibilities to this game; so just experiment and have fun.

Though do note; when doing large projects the game can start to lag though this is also dependent on PC specs so keep that in mind; performance will vary.

No ragdolls were harmed in the making of this review!
Runs great on my dell laptop with 8gbs of ram and no dedicated graphics card
Its a really fun game but %99 of the mods say "suspicious" even though they are probably not and im to scared to download them. And if you dont have mods, it gets very, very boring after a while.

Great physics game. So many workshop creations

best game ive everplayed better than melon playground btw and never play the fakes on edge

Fun with wires and signals, fluid, and dynamic systems.

Desperately needs multicore support for a future title.

really fun to mess around with, the gore is handled well and I love the guns and explosives. super well made game, I've seen tons of community contraptions that defy my very understanding of the mechanics!

!Spoilers!
I have 930 hours of time spent on this game, needless to say this is a really good game, best sandbox game I've played. There are lots of fun ways to kill or just have fun experimenting with ragdolls and machines. But there are also weird things hidden throughout, like hidden codes or rooms that lead to weird things outside of the game, like files or websites (Only one as far as I've looked), but I won't spoil anymore (I do suggest looking for the hidden things though).

I initally enjoyed this game, but it is a massive time waster for me, it's horribly optimized, especially for a 2D game and it cracks under the smallest amount of pressure even without addons. I have also had numerous infuriating and nonsensical glitches, like NPCs spawning dead, tearing themselves apart, getting instantly set on fire when they are spawned and dupes I made being broken because of mods that have nothing to do with it. I completely regret buying this game and being around it's community. The community around this game is toxic and many classic mods I loved were left unusable after the new update that is supossedly fighting a "virus." I understand the people who still enjoy the game and I envy the people who can see what I don't in it but I have lost all love for this game over the years.
The Game on its own is pretty fun, but the mods and modding community is what makes this game incredible

the game is good for many things modding, Dnd typ things, testing torther devices ect.
so if you don`d want to pay for this game, its definatly worth it
with 600 hours + i can say this game is super good and i love it and every gets to have fun when playing this game

**Why People Playground Is the Best Game Ever**

People Playground is one of the best games ever created because it gives players almost unlimited freedom. Unlike many games that force players to follow a strict story or complete specific objectives, People Playground allows players to create their own experiences. Whether someone wants to build machines, test physics, or simply experiment with different objects, the game encourages creativity in a way that very few games do.

One of the biggest reasons why People Playground stands out is its realistic physics system. The game simulates how objects interact with one another in an impressive way. Characters react differently depending on what happens to them, and every item in the game behaves according to the laws of physics. This attention to detail makes every experiment feel unique and entertaining.

Another reason why the game is so enjoyable is the massive variety of items available. Players can use weapons, vehicles, medical tools, machinery, and countless other objects. Because there are so many different combinations to try, it is difficult for the gameplay to become repetitive. There is always something new to discover or invent.

People Playground also rewards imagination. Instead of telling players exactly what to do, it provides them with the tools to create whatever scenarios they can think of. Some players design complex machines, while others build elaborate obstacle courses or recreate scenes from their favorite movies and games. The possibilities are almost endless.

The game's modding community contributes greatly to its success as well. Players can download thousands of modifications created by other fans, adding new characters, maps, items, and features. This constant flow of community-made content helps keep the game fresh and exciting long after the base game has been explored.

Another strength of People Playground is its simplicity. Although the game contains many advanced mechanics, the controls are easy to learn. New players can quickly understand the basics, while experienced players can spend hours mastering more complicated creations. This balance makes the game accessible to a wide audience.

The sandbox nature of People Playground also encourages problem-solving skills. Building functional machines often requires experimentation and critical thinking. Players must figure out how different components work together to achieve their desired results. In this way, the game can be both entertaining and mentally stimulating.

In addition, People Playground allows players to express themselves creatively without limitations. Some people use the game to tell stories, while others use it as an outlet for engineering ideas. The freedom to approach the game in different ways means that every player's experience can be completely different from someone else's.

The developers deserve praise for continuing to improve the game through updates and new features. Their support has helped maintain an active player base and has ensured that the game continues to evolve. This dedication demonstrates a commitment to providing players with the best possible experience.

In conclusion, People Playground can be considered one of the best games ever because of its creativity, realistic physics, extensive content, thriving modding community, and limitless replay value. It allows players to experiment, build, and explore in ways that few other games can match. For those who enjoy sandbox games that encourage imagination and innovation, People Playground truly earns its reputation as an exceptional gaming experience.

This is, ironically, a nice game to play for me.

I'm into that genre of game that doesn't have any defined path to go on, in People Playground, it is so easy to get pulled in because there isn't any pressure put on you, just a bunch of items that you get to mess about and experiment with, and the amount of mods you can add is insane because everyone is free to make them.

I love how this is practically just a place to test things that you could NEVER do IRL unless you were a war criminal, it sounds demented (and it probably is ;] ), but it's really nice to be able to unleash your heart out into destruction without destroying anyone's peace, it really makes me feel like I'm free to do anything I want, because I am.

Overall, 10/10; there are no major issues, which is good enough for me.
Best game for if you are bored, even if you're not bored, this still brightens up my day. One of the best workshop i've ever seen!
Its a fun game but gets a little laggy at times unless you have a like 30 or 4080 card, I do recomend

But over all i think this is an awesome game and that you will love it!

Lots of things to do 
You can get creative
Cons:
Game gets laggy quick depending on what you're doing 
Can be hard to learn what to do and how to do it, tutorial recommended
For what you get it is a little expensive sometimes (sales pretty regularly though)

Overall 7/10
